en: Have you ever heard of the phrase catnap? Ever wonder where it came from?
This idiom has been around since the early 1800s during the Victorian era. That is when
people started using the phrase catnap to describe short periods of sleep during the day, usually
referring to them dozing off in the sunlight while sitting in the garden for 10 to 20 minutes at a
time. Similar to what cats do, the biggest difference is cats sleep for 12 to 16 hours a day
so they take many naps. Nowadays we call them power naps. Experts say it is a great way to
restore energy and increase productivity. There have been many famous people who are religious
nappers like Thomas Edison, Ronald Reagan, Bill Clinton, and Albert Einstein to name a few.
I think we all deserve a catnap. Maybe our jobs will make a catnap room for us. I mean if it
helped really smart people maybe it would only be making us better which is good for the business
right? Well gotta go take a catnap.
